refnamediv.refpurpose: Display selected DICOM slices with optional metadata panels.

refsynopsisdiv.title: Syntax
refsynopsisdiv.synopsis: view = dicomshow(filename) view = dicomshow(filename, slices, infoMode) view = dicomshow(volume, slices, infoMode, info)

listitem.para: DICOM filename. The file is loaded using dicomread.
Scalar or vector of 1-based frame indices. The middle frame is used by default.
"none" hides DICOM annotations, "slice" places information on each slice, and "general" adds one shared information panel. A boolean maps true to "slice" and false to "none".
Metadata structure returned by dicomread. It is optional for a numeric volume.
Structure containing the figure handle, selected slices, information mode, metadata, and displayed uint8 frames.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Uses one consistent DICOM window across selected frames. Window Center and Window Width are honored when present; otherwise the finite volume range is used. MONOCHROME1 images are inverted for display.

Multiple frames are arranged with Scilab subplots. General information includes non-identifying acquisition fields such as modality, dimensions, series, spacing, transfer syntax, and rescale values. Patient name and ID are never added to the figure automatically.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: Example 1 displays four frames and one shared information panel.
Example 2 reads once, then displays selected frames with per-slice information.
Example 3 turns off all slice annotations.

tr.td: DICOM slice viewer with selectable metadata modes introduced.
